open_hooks_to_carry: The new living arrangements must feel materially reduced but not symbolically purified.
Leela's handoff should be awkward, ordinary, and fully manual.
prev_tail_excerpt:
...oute halo opened. No polite vibration suggested the fastest legal crossing for a protected family unit. Cars went by and did not know who they were.

Ben stood there with a duffel strap digging into his shoulder, a grocery bag numbing his fingers, and his whole former life packed so badly it clanked.

He felt terror then, clean and undressed. Not the branded version. Not anxiety translated into productivity. Real terror. Where they were sleeping. How to tell Leela. What happened when the co-op formally locked them out. Whether medication refills could survive a benefits lapse. Whether his joke reflex would come back at exactly the wrong time and make Nita want to hit him. Whether freedom was just a respectable word people used before they had to price winter coats twice.

The light changed.

Nita looked at him. Her face was wind-reddened, tired, and so stripped of managerial polish it hurt to see. "We go to my mother's first," she said. "We don't pretend this is noble. We do tonight. Then tomorrow."

"Okay," he said.

It was the first agreement they had made all day that did not feel outsourced.

Pilar pushed forward with the hand truck. Ben and Nita went with her into the crosswalk, the boxes rattling, the pot striking cardboard, the cracked orange leaking sweet over cold spinach and paper. Behind them the building held its heat and its cameras and its sponsored groceries and whatever story the systems would now attempt. Ahead there was only the ordinary street, filthy snow at the curb, no route opened for them, and enough space, finally, for a human mistake to remain one.
state_deltas_required: The family must build a workable post-optimization life with less money, less convenience, and no certainty that manual arrangements will hold.
Nita and Ben achieve a smaller, poorer, genuinely human co-parenting arrangement outside the old household script.
